Establish monitoring and evaluation framework, including timelines and impact assessment.

Proposals must show convergence with similar schemes to avoid overlap
Avoid unnecessary establishment and administrative expenses; focus on resource convergence
Common norms should be followed across similar schemes for better coordination
Proposals must include Aadhaar authentication for beneficiary identification and fund disbursement
Incorporate cashless and electronic transactions in scheme design

45c8d114d32bbdd475ca316abf0f7498b013ccbe.rule_statement: Submit evaluation report with major findings and comments on observations
Propose major changes from earlier scheme with justification
Report any major changes in costing norms

Cases not covered by specific limits will be decided on their merits.
Recovery of wrongful/excess payments to government servants is mandated, with few exceptions.

1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.rule_statement: Recovery of excess payments to employees is permissible unless it causes undue hardship or is inequitable
1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.ministry_must_show: Justification for recovery without causing hardship

1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.rule_statement: Government actions must be based on equity and good conscience regarding employee livelihoods
1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.ministry_must_show: Assessment of fairness in recovery actions

1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.rule_statement: Repeated exemptions from recovery indicate iniquitous actions and may require judicial intervention
1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.ministry_must_show: Documentation of past exemptions and their justifications

1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.rule_statement: Recovery actions must not violate the principle of equality before the law as per Article 14
1d7280dcc7c27a1584b64ff34ad65b7eddc7b248.ministry_must_show: Compliance with Article 14 in recovery processes

49fe021e9ae029b9bc0d9c59a58ed27a8697daea.rule_statement: Recovery of dues from retired employees or those retiring within one year is mandated

d980741d645d371bef38c76e7463be0d5f85f513.rule_statement: Recovery from employees for excess payment over five years requires prior order issuance

97a102af2318f3ec965fc49921596c19390f4567.rule_statement: Recovery from employees wrongfully assigned higher duties is required if they were paid for it
97a102af2318f3ec965fc49921596c19390f4567.ministry_must_show: Evidence of wrongful duty assignment and payment

97a102af2318f3ec965fc49921596c19390f4567.rule_statement: Court may prevent recovery if deemed iniquitous, harsh, or arbitrary

5730063a85bb23d464308b30630c0f289bc8e4dd.rule_statement: Address wrongful/excess payments to government servants per Supreme Court decision
Waiver of recovery requires express approval from Department of Expenditure
5730063a85bb23d464308b30630c0f289bc8e4dd.ministry_must_show: Obtain DoE approval for any waiver

649ca0047591301c4ad5922435fa67e4a813e3a4.rule_statement: Central Sector Schemes must be implemented by designated agencies of Central Ministries/Departments

5d7ac87993b317c0f2eba312f1d3b423a9bd9f60.rule_statement: Centrally Sponsored Schemes funded by Central and State Governments as per approved sharing pattern
Projects involve one-time expenditure for creating capital assets with potential financial returns
Ministries must rationalize schemes to avoid resource dilution and ensure effective outcomes

3da78067c3105860098e7b4b121dc71bbc72d4aa.rule_statement: No new Scheme or Sub-Scheme without prior in-principle approval from DoE, except Budget Speech announcements

2e1d3394282bef09a6e2a8bd65442dcf55f58ff6.rule_statement: Budget Estimates must align with approved scheme architecture; deviations require prior agreement with DoE

0825f1a20eab5cfe23926825fad10e4401483ee4.rule_statement: Administrative Ministries must merge, restructure, or drop redundant schemes continuously.
0825f1a20eab5cfe23926825fad10e4401483ee4.ministry_must_show: Evidence of scheme evaluation and actions taken

0825f1a20eab5cfe23926825fad10e4401483ee4.rule_statement: DoE can merge, restructure, or drop schemes in consultation with Administrative Departments.
New schemes must include a Concept Paper and stakeholder consultations for in-principle approval.
0825f1a20eab5cfe23926825fad10e4401483ee4.ministry_must_show: Submit Concept Paper with stakeholder feedback

0825f1a20eab5cfe23926825fad10e4401483ee4.rule_statement: Ongoing schemes need rationalization and a Detailed Paper for appraisal.
Project preparation must start with a Feasibility Report to ensure techno-economic soundness.
No new bodies should be established without Cabinet approval, regardless of outlay.
Delegation of powers for Original Cost Estimates varies by project cost thresholds.
0825f1a20eab5cfe23926825fad10e4401483ee4.ministry_must_show: Compliance with appraisal and approval thresholds

7c02c3a996da137978d0cf8b7da47d5c29ec9090.rule_statement: Separate orders from the Department of Economic Affairs govern appraisal and approval of PPP projects
7c02c3a996da137978d0cf8b7da47d5c29ec9090.ministry_must_show: Compliance with Department of Economic Affairs orders

42d1ec6feed02a228a1bacb34acf5df3d8cb1fce.rule_statement: Cost increases up to 20% due to various reasons are approved under original estimates by Secretary with Financial Adviser's concurrence
42d1ec6feed02a228a1bacb34acf5df3d8cb1fce.ministry_must_show: Secretary's approval with Financial Adviser's concurrence

42d1ec6feed02a228a1bacb34acf5df3d8cb1fce.rule_statement: Increases beyond 20% must be reviewed by a Revised Cost Committee before further approval
42d1ec6feed02a228a1bacb34acf5df3d8cb1fce.ministry_must_show: Submission to Revised Cost Committee for reasons and remedial suggestions

42d1ec6feed02a228a1bacb34acf5df3d8cb1fce.rule_statement: Revised Cost Committee must identify reasons for cost increases and suggest remedial measures
42d1ec6feed02a228a1bacb34acf5df3d8cb1fce.ministry_must_show: Recommendations for fresh appraisal and approval

2d79f795806860e90d9b419e2b1ff5e51c67cf7a.rule_statement: Pre-investment activities up to Rs. 100 crore can be approved by Secretary with Financial Adviser's concurrence
2d79f795806860e90d9b419e2b1ff5e51c67cf7a.ministry_must_show: Financial resources availability and in-principle approval

2d79f795806860e90d9b419e2b1ff5e51c67cf7a.rule_statement: For pre-investment activities above Rs. 100 crore, follow prescribed appraisal and approval procedures
All Ministries must conduct outcome reviews and resubmit schemes at the end of the Twelfth Plan period
2d79f795806860e90d9b419e2b1ff5e51c67cf7a.ministry_must_show: Outcome review report and resubmission of schemes

2d79f795806860e90d9b419e2b1ff5e51c67cf7a.rule_statement: Ministries must prepare an output-outcome framework for each scheme with NITI Aayog's approval
Third-party evaluation process initiated by NITI Aayog for scheme extension across Finance Commission Cycles

8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.rule_statement: Evaluation arrangements must be defined; continuation of schemes requires third-party evaluation.
8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.ministry_must_show: Submit third-party evaluation report for continuation

8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.rule_statement: An Executive Summary must precede the document, summarizing key points.
8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.ministry_must_show: Attach Executive Summary with main elements

8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.rule_statement: Time frames for various appraisal stages must be adhered to; delays require escalation.
8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.ministry_must_show: Trigger mechanism for delays must be established

8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.rule_statement: Administrative structure for scheme implementation should avoid creating new entities.
Monitoring framework and arrangements for audits must be indicated.
8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.ministry_must_show: Provide details of monitoring and audit arrangements

8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.rule_statement: Third-party evaluation is necessary for scheme extension from one period to another.
8a7238cb6bae6dc9791edbc65670fa88876f348b.ministry_must_show: Submit evaluation report for extension approval

bf0dda8fb1755dc68485ca7915f2a08862b5bdd6.rule_statement: Title of the project must be clearly stated
Proposed cost of the project must be outlined
Proposed timelines for project completion must be provided
Indicate if the project is part of a scheme or standalone
Confirm if financial resources for the project are secured
Feasibility Report and/or Detailed Project Report must be prepared
Specify if the proposal is an Original or Revised Cost Estimate

490089fddd7f8953456ccfd94642502a505ef687.rule_statement: Revised Cost Estimates require a meeting of the Revised Cost Committee and addressing its recommendations
490089fddd7f8953456ccfd94642502a505ef687.ministry_must_show: Confirmation of committee meeting and recommendation compliance

4f070909d89c1e821d613a87993e70fdb13d4f17.rule_statement: Clearly state the aims and objectives of the project
4f070909d89c1e821d613a87993e70fdb13d4f17.ministry_must_show: Document outlining project aims and objectives

4f070909d89c1e821d613a87993e70fdb13d4f17.rule_statement: Provide year-wise outputs and deliverables in a table format
4f070909d89c1e821d613a87993e70fdb13d4f17.ministry_must_show: Table of year-wise outputs and deliverables

ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.rule_statement: Define measurable indicators for project outcomes for impact assessment post-completion.
ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.ministry_must_show: List of measurable indicators and baseline data

ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.rule_statement: Provide cost estimates and scheduled duration for the project, with basis for estimates not older than one year.
ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.ministry_must_show: Cost estimates and schedule with reference dates

ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.rule_statement: Include details of land cost and rehabilitation/resettlement if land acquisition is needed.
Specify proposed expenditure on pre-investment activities with details activity-wise.
Include price escalation rates in cost estimates for the project duration.
Indicate any foreign exchange elements and potential exchange rate risks in the project.
Attach variation analysis and report from the Revised Cost Committee for revised cost estimates.
Indicate sources of project finance including budgetary and external aid.
Specify cost components shared by state governments, local bodies, or private parties.
Support availability of internal resources with projections and deployment plans.
Indicate funding tie-ups for loan components with terms and conditions.
Indicate if government support/loan funds have been secured.
Provide leveraging details including debt-equity and interest coverage ratios.
Mention legacy arrangements for maintenance of assets post-project completion.
Calculate financial internal rate of return for projects with identifiable financial returns, using a 10% hurdle rate.
ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.ministry_must_show: Financial internal rate of return calculation

ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.rule_statement: Calculate economic rate of return for projects with identifiable economic returns, considering both financial and economic returns.
Indicate measurable benefits/outcomes for projects without quantifiable financial or economic returns.
Indicate mandatory approvals/clearances required from various bodies in a tabular form.
Indicate the administrative structure for project implementation, avoiding new structures.
Specify manpower requirements and submit separate proposals for new posts.
Provide brief details if outsourcing services or hiring consultants is intended.
Indicate the Project Management/Implementing Agency(s) and any payable agency charges.
ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.ministry_must_show: Details of implementing agency and charges

ff2d7d857a76f776764dc7d019c10fa6d2dd0fe6.rule_statement: Indicate mode of implementation for individual works (e.g., PPP, EPC).
Provide timelines of activities in PERT/Bar Chart with critical milestones.
Indicate monitoring framework including MIS and audit arrangements.
Specify arrangements for impact assessment after project completion.
Summarize comments from NITI Aayog, DoE, and other bodies and how they were used to improve the proposal.
Attach an Executive Summary along with the Feasibility Report/Detailed Project Report for the project.

c9b7b3c8f99658990b44ed82cb0ecec735eaf3d2.rule_statement: Continuation of any scheme post-Fourteenth Finance Commission depends on evaluation results by NITI Aayog
c9b7b3c8f99658990b44ed82cb0ecec735eaf3d2.ministry_must_show: Submit evaluation results to justify scheme continuation

c9b7b3c8f99658990b44ed82cb0ecec735eaf3d2.rule_statement: New companies or bodies creation requires Cabinet approval to prevent unnecessary liabilities
c9b7b3c8f99658990b44ed82cb0ecec735eaf3d2.ministry_must_show: Proposal for new bodies with justification

c9b7b3c8f99658990b44ed82cb0ecec735eaf3d2.rule_statement: CEE constituted to appraise proposals for new bodies and examine establishment liabilities
c9b7b3c8f99658990b44ed82cb0ecec735eaf3d2.ministry_must_show: Submit proposals to CEE for review

daf5e3454bc3af872ab85e58338e1f80e16a708d.rule_statement: Recurring expenditure should be minimized by utilizing internal resources to lessen budgetary burden.
Creation of new posts must be processed on file; no pre-investment activities without DoE approval unless budget announced.
daf5e3454bc3af872ab85e58338e1f80e16a708d.ministry_must_show: DoE in-principle approval or budget announcement

daf5e3454bc3af872ab85e58338e1f80e16a708d.rule_statement: All matters for Cabinet Security Committee must be forwarded to the Personnel Division of DoE.
Integrated Finance of respective Ministry/Department acts as Secretariat for CEE.
Department of Expenditure has the authority to relax any provisions of these guidelines.

0813be90d9a1bc742ea829ba0cc742c7a2b4e441.rule_statement: Railways can create non-gazetted revenue posts for crew as part of crew review exercise
0813be90d9a1bc742ea829ba0cc742c7a2b4e441.ministry_must_show: Compliance with crew review exercise outcomes

8fffd06268a5278c1a1a8b9e0870e1db0dc5efe4.rule_statement: Continuation of delegated powers to DIB for post location transfer and exemption from deemed-abolition clauses

2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.rule_statement: Transfer of posts up to Major General requires concurrence from Defence Finance and approval from Raksha Mantri
2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.ministry_must_show: Concurrence from Secretary (Defence Finance) and Raksha Mantri approval

2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.rule_statement: Transfers above Major General require Finance Minister's approval
Transfers of posts among Missions/Posts abroad are permitted
2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.ministry_must_show: Compliance with relevant guidelines for transfers

2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.rule_statement: Creation of Group A, B, C & D posts in Delhi requires positive Balance from Current Revenues
2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.ministry_must_show: Evidence of positive Balance from Current Revenues

2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.rule_statement: Continuation of powers for creating scientific and technical posts for 5 more years; JS level and above need Cabinet Committee on Security approval
2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.ministry_must_show: Cabinet Committee on Security approval for JS and above

2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.rule_statement: Checklist for creation of posts includes details like name, number, pay level, and justification
Checklist for revival of posts includes details like name, number, pay level, and justification for revival
Details of posts proposed to be surrendered must be provided under specified guidelines
2188a733f88ae0501ffcb6986f6e7852e84c38e2.ministry_must_show: Details of posts with level of pay for surrender

eb2b2875434c55e100ab2a05bee7bb0f4582f5e4.rule_statement: Provide detailed sanctioned strength by category, including pay levels and vacancy status
eb2b2875434c55e100ab2a05bee7bb0f4582f5e4.ministry_must_show: List of positions, pay levels, and vacancy dates

fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.rule_statement: Deputy Secretary and above entitled to office telephone with STD facility; others need consultation for functional requirements
fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.ministry_must_show: Consultation with Financial Advisers for lower ranks

fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.rule_statement: ISD facility allowed only for Administrative Secretaries on official telephones
fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.ministry_must_show: Approval for ISD facility for Administrative Secretaries

fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.rule_statement: ISD facility for lower ranks decided by Administrative Secretary with Financial Adviser consultation
Administrative Secretary can provide STD facility to officers below Deputy Secretary on functional basis with caution
Financial Advisors must submit half-yearly report on ISD facility approvals
Deputy Secretary and above entitled to one residential landline telephone with STD facility
fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.ministry_must_show: Documentation of entitlement for residential landline

fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.rule_statement: Residential telephone for lower ranks limited to 25% of sanctioned strength
ISD facility not allowed on residential telephones
Secretary and equivalent entitled to reimbursement for one mobile handset up to Rs.25,000 once during tenure
Reimbursement limits for telephone charges vary by designation, with specified monthly ceilings
No SIM/data-card provided by office
Reimbursement covers all types of connections within prescribed ceilings
Reimbursement requires submission of bills/receipts by officers
Only one reimbursement allowed for shared residential landline between entitled officers
Reimbursement for mobile restricted to the officer registered with the connection
Excess expenditure up to 30% can be reimbursed for certain officers with justification and approvals
No telephone reimbursement during leave or training exceeding one month
Officials abroad may receive SIM card from Mission/Embassy or reimbursement up to Rs.2000 or Rs.1000 per day
fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.direction: SIM or reimbursement for abroad visits
fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.ministry_must_show: Documentation of SIM provision or reimbursement claim

fac68f95c047de906cd3ce3f96c90962356c0fcf.rule_statement: No mobile phone facility during training periods
Ceiling on furnishings for Ministers' offices revised and must be adhered to
Proposals for conferences with expenditure above Rs. 40 lakhs must be referred to DoE

5b0a0f5ea0776a6fc28c48ff9a3c241892bc96ae.rule_statement: Proposals for expenditure of Rs. 40 Lakh or less for events can be approved by the Ministry with Financial Adviser consultation
Approval from Minister in Charge is required for the proposals
Political clearance from Ministry of External Affairs must be obtained for the proposals
5b0a0f5ea0776a6fc28c48ff9a3c241892bc96ae.ministry_must_show: Clearance from Ministry of External Affairs

5b0a0f5ea0776a6fc28c48ff9a3c241892bc96ae.rule_statement: Clearance from Ministry of Home Affairs for security is required where applicable
5b0a0f5ea0776a6fc28c48ff9a3c241892bc96ae.ministry_must_show: Security clearance from Ministry of Home Affairs

378988411293eb9fb70fbf8363af8c414cdcd213.rule_statement: Proposals over Rs. 40 lakh for international events must be referred to DoE with necessary approvals
378988411293eb9fb70fbf8363af8c414cdcd213.ministry_must_show: Minister's approval, political clearance, and security clearance

00603157629a26eaf655c67c62f91ff1aca5f85f.rule_statement: Travel/accommodation costs for foreign participants must be minimized; utmost economy required
In-principle approval from Minister-in-charge required well in advance before events
Priority for conferences arising from international agreements; others only if budget allows
Complete all preparations for conferences in advance to avoid last-minute issues
Administrative arrangements must follow Cabinet Secretary's approval or delegated powers
00603157629a26eaf655c67c62f91ff1aca5f85f.ministry_must_show: Cabinet Secretary's approval or delegation evidence

00603157629a26eaf655c67c62f91ff1aca5f85f.rule_statement: Proposals under Rs. 40 lakh can be decided by Ministry/Department with Financial Adviser's consultation
Proposals above Rs. 40 lakh for Indian-only conferences need DoE approval after Secretary's consent
00603157629a26eaf655c67c62f91ff1aca5f85f.ministry_must_show: Secretary's approval before referral to DoE

00603157629a26eaf655c67c62f91ff1aca5f85f.rule_statement: Autonomous Bodies can approve conferences without government funds; if funds needed, refer to DoE if above Rs. 40 lakh
00603157629a26eaf655c67c62f91ff1aca5f85f.ministry_must_show: Evidence of revenue generation or funding need

8c4747dfdd87bbeca876b59c968f4d24aa6dbe21.rule_statement: Discourage holding exhibitions abroad unless for trade promotion or 'Brand India' projection
8c4747dfdd87bbeca876b59c968f4d24aa6dbe21.ministry_must_show: Justification for event necessity and alignment with objectives

8c4747dfdd87bbeca876b59c968f4d24aa6dbe21.rule_statement: Identify a Nodal Ministry/Department for events involving multiple ministries
8c4747dfdd87bbeca876b59c968f4d24aa6dbe21.ministry_must_show: Nodal ministry designation and coordination plan

b876de7383bb05ecc1a58f6812e1503b75e52adc.rule_statement: Proposals must be sent to DoE at least one month before the event through the Financial Adviser
Ensure necessary clearances from relevant ministries and competent authority are obtained before submission
b876de7383bb05ecc1a58f6812e1503b75e52adc.ministry_must_show: All required clearances documented in the file

b876de7383bb05ecc1a58f6812e1503b75e52adc.rule_statement: Proposals without necessary clearances will be returned unprocessed by DoE

39d37f76b96cc962f85df7a28ed29ceaf22240d2.rule_statement: Ensure sufficient budget provision before processing proposals and referring to DoE
39d37f76b96cc962f85df7a28ed29ceaf22240d2.ministry_must_show: Clear indication of budget provision in proposals

713075736a555cea5c3133992af6975909e5fddb.rule_statement: Proposals must be submitted within stipulated timelines; delays require a Delay Report
713075736a555cea5c3133992af6975909e5fddb.ministry_must_show: Delay Report approved by Administrative Secretary

6b345bb76b4b6f127b4d4e42fae5a2c47086941d.rule_statement: Conferences in Five Star Hotels banned unless for specific high-level official engagements
6b345bb76b4b6f127b4d4e42fae5a2c47086941d.ministry_must_show: Justification for any deviation from this rule

220584f46a4dbdafba6bef4f28599bb68aea9095.rule_statement: Ministries/Departments must not seek ex post-facto approval as it may be rejected
220584f46a4dbdafba6bef4f28599bb68aea9095.ministry_must_show: Evidence of advance planning and approvals

220584f46a4dbdafba6bef4f28599bb68aea9095.rule_statement: Emphasis on adequate advance planning and obtaining all necessary approvals
220584f46a4dbdafba6bef4f28599bb68aea9095.ministry_must_show: Documentation of all requisite approvals and clearances

b2f577b194d6f409772733f0dd4e3c5070ca4fb7.rule_statement: Despite increased expenditure ceilings, Ministries must ensure maximum economy in public spending

cbe6004a11f71765d178b7c25f1eef6ee3c7d033.rule_statement: Ministries/Departments must upload foreign visit data on FVMS using provided credentials
cbe6004a11f71765d178b7c25f1eef6ee3c7d033.ministry_must_show: Timely and accurate data on foreign visits

d462fb39baeac4b7a96c3938ae99141d86f0cbe9.rule_statement: Each Ministry/Department must prepare a Quarterly Rolling Plan for foreign tours every 3 months
d462fb39baeac4b7a96c3938ae99141d86f0cbe9.ministry_must_show: Upload QRP on FVMS for review

d462fb39baeac4b7a96c3938ae99141d86f0cbe9.rule_statement: The QRP will be reviewed monthly with an additional month added each time
Only unavoidable essential foreign visits may be included in the QRP

9d6e637c524ac98afa52c42706d9b8bb4a804891.rule_statement: Delegation size must be minimized based on expertise and available manpower
9d6e637c524ac98afa52c42706d9b8bb4a804891.ministry_must_show: Rationale for delegation size and expertise

9d6e637c524ac98afa52c42706d9b8bb4a804891.rule_statement: No foreign visit if objectives can be met via letters or tele/video conferencing
9d6e637c524ac98afa52c42706d9b8bb4a804891.ministry_must_show: Evidence of alternative communication methods used

418fc36c1d0d1a86d816193354e1a6f2a72b8ac4.rule_statement: Visit duration must be minimized; appropriate functional level officers should be deputed
418fc36c1d0d1a86d816193354e1a6f2a72b8ac4.ministry_must_show: Justification for visit duration and officer level

bfc89658e8b9aba2cdd7f4cfa2288ed04c2ed995.rule_statement: Foreign visits limited to 5 working days; longer visits or larger delegations need SCoS approval
bfc89658e8b9aba2cdd7f4cfa2288ed04c2ed995.ministry_must_show: Submit delegation details for SCoS review

a1274dfa1283c323b9e0d4286faa1cabcfa755f3.rule_statement: No officer may undertake more than 4 official visits abroad annually
Secretary/equivalent must submit proposals for visits exceeding 4 to the Prime Minister via SCoS
a1274dfa1283c323b9e0d4286faa1cabcfa755f3.ministry_must_show: Proposal for excess visits to SCoS

a1274dfa1283c323b9e0d4286faa1cabcfa755f3.rule_statement: Officers below Secretary level must submit proposals for visits exceeding 4 to SCoS for approval
a1274dfa1283c323b9e0d4286faa1cabcfa755f3.ministry_must_show: Proposal for excess visits to SCoS

a1274dfa1283c323b9e0d4286faa1cabcfa755f3.rule_statement: Ministries/Departments should train 2-3 officers on relevant subjects to reduce repetitive visits
a1274dfa1283c323b9e0d4286faa1cabcfa755f3.ministry_must_show: Training plan for officers on relevant subjects

d0da49a22cf7886525fe07f3ac265e5c3394733f.rule_statement: Participation in international events discouraged; only essential officers may attend
Promote coordinated presence of 'Brand India' instead of individual stalls by Departments
Identify a nodal Department to lead coordination for international events with MEA
d0da49a22cf7886525fe07f3ac265e5c3394733f.ministry_must_show: Nodal Department's designation and consultation details

Other financial sanctions should be issued as an Order with a copy to the Accounts Officer
Non-recurring expenditure can be sanctioned by signing the bill or voucher instead of a separate sanction
1ca2e55960378464daac4bbf48ed3d788f8aa823.ministry_must_show: Signed bill or voucher as sanction

1ca2e55960378464daac4bbf48ed3d788f8aa823.rule_statement: Sanctions with Internal Finance Wing or Finance Ministry concurrence must be communicated to the Accounts Officer
1ca2e55960378464daac4bbf48ed3d788f8aa823.ministry_must_show: Compliance with Delegation of Financial Powers Rules

1ca2e55960378464daac4bbf48ed3d788f8aa823.rule_statement: Sanctions with concurrence from specific departments must specify the concurrence details in the order
1ca2e55960378464daac4bbf48ed3d788f8aa823.ministry_must_show: Details of concurrence and communication date

842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.rule_statement: Sanction orders must state expenditure amounts in both words and figures
842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.ministry_must_show: Sanction orders with amounts clearly stated

842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.rule_statement: Sanctions by Head of Department can be communicated by an authorized Gazetted Officer
842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.ministry_must_show: Sanction communication signed by authorized officer

842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.rule_statement: Orders for pay additions must include reasons for the grant
842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.ministry_must_show: Summary of reasons for pay additions

842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.rule_statement: Orders from Union Territory Departments must be sent directly to Audit if accounts not separated
842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.ministry_must_show: Ensure orders reach Audit authority directly

842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.rule_statement: Sanctions involving Central Government references must include a specific clause
All General Financial Orders must be supplied to the Comptroller and Auditor General
842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.ministry_must_show: Copies of General Financial Orders sent to CAG

842ba42417b58d9b88852f3a2f214363a56974e7.rule_statement: Sanctions lapse if no payment is made within 12 months unless renewed

080566a18981af59d56356061d4cb6ab332182c6.rule_statement: Sanction lapses when the prescribed period in regulations or sanction expires
Expenditure sanctioned for a specific financial year lapses at the end of that year
Any loss of public money or property must be reported immediately to higher authorities
080566a18981af59d56356061d4cb6ab332182c6.ministry_must_show: Loss report to higher authority and audit officer

080566a18981af59d56356061d4cb6ab332182c6.rule_statement: Serious irregularities must be reported to Financial Adviser and Controller General of Accounts
080566a18981af59d56356061d4cb6ab332182c6.ministry_must_show: Notification of irregularities to relevant authorities

080566a18981af59d56356061d4cb6ab332182c6.rule_statement: Sanction for permanent establishment additions does not lapse if not drawn by concerned officers
Remission of disallowances and writing off overpayments must follow Delegation of Financial Powers Rules
Loss reports must be made at two stages as per sub-rules (1) and (2)

23c5b06c2f9814c9eb89c9d39357379873407540.rule_statement: Initial report of loss must be made as soon as suspicion arises
23c5b06c2f9814c9eb89c9d39357379873407540.ministry_must_show: Initial report detailing suspicion of loss

23c5b06c2f9814c9eb89c9d39357379873407540.rule_statement: Final report after investigation must detail nature, extent of loss, and recovery prospects
Complete report must reach Head of Department for final disposal; unresolved cases go to Finance Ministry
Loss through misappropriation can be redrawn on receipt pending investigation with competent authority's approval
Loss due to culpability of officials borne by respective Government Department; recoveries credited back
Loss from erroneous cheque issues or irregular accounting must be reported to Controller General of Accounts
Loss of Government property over ₹50,000 due to fire, theft, fraud must be reported to Police for investigation

4dcf30092d79bcac31d316993ce8e3bf9b0b3cea.rule_statement: Report loss of immovable property over ₹50,000 due to natural causes immediately to Government
After inquiry, send detailed loss report to Government and copy to Audit and Accounts Officers
Officers are personally liable for losses due to their fraud or negligence
Ensure prompt action on detection, reporting, and disposal of loss cases
4dcf30092d79bcac31d316993ce8e3bf9b0b3cea.ministry_must_show: Records of actions taken at each stage

4dcf30092d79bcac31d316993ce8e3bf9b0b3cea.rule_statement: Financial year runs from April 1 to March 31 of the following year
Finance Minister must present Annual Financial Statement to Parliament before financial year starts

c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.rule_statement: Railways' receipts and expenditure included in the General Budget since 2017-18
c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.ministry_must_show: Align Railway budget with General Budget

c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.rule_statement: Budget preparation and submission provisions are in Articles 112 to 116 of the Constitution
Ministry of Finance to issue budget preparation guidelines; all must comply
Budget must include revenue estimates, expenditure estimates, debt servicing, and prescribed information
c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.ministry_must_show: Complete budget with all specified estimates

c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.rule_statement: Detailed receipt estimates to be prepared for each Major Head with past three years' actuals
c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.ministry_must_show: Break-up of estimates and past actuals

c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.rule_statement: Non-tax revenues collected by all Ministries/Departments are vital for government revenue
Ministries/Departments must identify and publish user charges on their websites
c3dd8325c138a686bc7b4f803b4998aa04326e8d.ministry_must_show: List of user charges on website

6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.rule_statement: User charges must cover current service costs and provide reasonable returns on capital investment
Any deviation from cost recovery norms must be recorded with justifications
6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.ministry_must_show: Record of reasons for lower charges

6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.rule_statement: User charge rates should be linked to price indices and reviewed every three years
6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.ministry_must_show: Review schedule and linkage to indices

6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.rule_statement: All Ministries/Departments must migrate to e-Receipts for non-tax revenue collection
Expenditure estimates must separate sums for Charged and voted expenditures as per Constitution
Estimates must distinguish between revenue and capital account expenditures
6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.ministry_must_show: Classification of revenue vs capital expenditures

6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.rule_statement: Detailed expenditure estimates must be prepared up to the final unit of appropriation
Scheme-related expenditures must be processed in consultation with the Budget Division
6ce3cd954c206357f8f6b1cbf9d32e51a0ed3827.ministry_must_show: Evidence of consultation with Budget Division

c9bebb2cd22bc13fd56b8096bfd997be91110c5b.rule_statement: Revised and Budget Estimates must be scrutinized and approved before forwarding to Budget Division
Expenditure estimates needing Lok Sabha vote must be in Demand for Grants format
Typically, one Demand for Grant per Ministry, but large ones may have multiple demands
c9bebb2cd22bc13fd56b8096bfd997be91110c5b.ministry_must_show: Justification for multiple demands if applicable

c9bebb2cd22bc13fd56b8096bfd997be91110c5b.rule_statement: Main Demand for Grants presented by Ministry of Finance; detailed demands by concerned Ministries
c9bebb2cd22bc13fd56b8096bfd997be91110c5b.ministry_must_show: Detailed demands submitted on approved dates

c9bebb2cd22bc13fd56b8096bfd997be91110c5b.rule_statement: Annual Financial Statement and Demands for Grants format set by Finance Ministry; no changes without approval
Expenditure heads in Demands for Grants must be prescribed by Finance Ministry with Administrative Ministry input
Major head provisions in Detailed Demands must match those in Budget Division's Demands for Grants

faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.rule_statement: Estimates of receipts and expenditure must be scrutinized by the Budget Division of the Ministry of Finance
faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.ministry_must_show: Submit estimates for review to Budget Division

faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.rule_statement: Departments must control expenditure against sanctioned grants and appropriations
Grants or appropriations can only cover charges for the current financial year
faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.ministry_must_show: Ensure all charges are within the financial year

faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.rule_statement: No expenditure exceeding authorized grant without supplementary approval or contingency fund advance
faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.ministry_must_show: Obtain necessary approvals for excess expenditure

faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.rule_statement: Controlling and Disbursing Officers must follow specified procedures for effective expenditure control
faa3b58007fe9b916d93bcac5781244fa6f11937.ministry_must_show: Adhere to outlined expenditure control procedures

e735850c0ccb30518a6e02e78ce4a5afb841870d.rule_statement: DDOs must prepare separate bills for charged and voted expenditure
Each bill must include complete account classifications from major to object head
Charges in a single bill across multiple object heads must be accurately distributed
DDOs must maintain separate registers in Form GFR 5 for each minor or sub-head
Monthly entries from GFR 5 register must be sent to the Head of Department by the 3rd day of each month
e735850c0ccb30518a6e02e78ce4a5afb841870d.ministry_must_show: Copy of monthly entries or nil statement

e735850c0ccb30518a6e02e78ce4a5afb841870d.rule_statement: Controlling Officer must maintain a broadsheet in Form GFR 6 to monitor returns
Controlling Officer must examine returns from DDOs and ensure compliance

19e2705059e48adaee8040308469a9a0032bc98c.rule_statement: Controlling Officer must verify returns from Disbursing Officers for accuracy and compliance
19e2705059e48adaee8040308469a9a0032bc98c.ministry_must_show: Corrected returns with justification for any discrepancies

19e2705059e48adaee8040308469a9a0032bc98c.rule_statement: Compile monthly expenditure statement in Form GFR 8 after verifying returns
19e2705059e48adaee8040308469a9a0032bc98c.ministry_must_show: Completed Form GFR 8 showing total expenditure

19e2705059e48adaee8040308469a9a0032bc98c.rule_statement: Head of Department and Accounts Officer must reconcile monthly account figures
PAOs must provide DDOs with monthly expenditure extracts from control register
19e2705059e48adaee8040308469a9a0032bc98c.ministry_must_show: Extracts showing classified expenditure and progressive figures

a050786f7a14158a320f8213f52dfdcee885e731.rule_statement: DDOs must tally figures from PAOs with monthly expenditure, excluding book adjustments, and investigate discrepancies
a050786f7a14158a320f8213f52dfdcee885e731.ministry_must_show: Certificate of agreement with PAO figures by month-end

a050786f7a14158a320f8213f52dfdcee885e731.rule_statement: PAOs must send monthly expenditure statements to Heads of Departments for comparison and reconciliation
a050786f7a14158a320f8213f52dfdcee885e731.ministry_must_show: Comparison with GFR 8 and reconciliation of differences

a050786f7a14158a320f8213f52dfdcee885e731.rule_statement: Maintain a Liability Register for effective expenditure control
Heads of Departments must estimate monthly savings or excesses and regularize them per Rule 62
a050786f7a14158a320f8213f52dfdcee885e731.ministry_must_show: Estimation report on savings or excesses

f05c5543019f4c5b1c274cb62f544079d8c3a11f.rule_statement: Authority administering grant/appropriation is responsible for controlling expenditure; Accounts Officer reports discrepancies
Payments exceeding budget provisions require specific approval from Chief Accounting Authority
f05c5543019f4c5b1c274cb62f544079d8c3a11f.ministry_must_show: Chief Accounting Authority's approval for excess

f05c5543019f4c5b1c274cb62f544079d8c3a11f.rule_statement: Financial Advisers must ensure fund availability before approving excess expenditure
f05c5543019f4c5b1c274cb62f544079d8c3a11f.ministry_must_show: Confirmation of funds via Re-appropriation/Supplementary Demands

f05c5543019f4c5b1c274cb62f544079d8c3a11f.rule_statement: Departments must surrender anticipated savings to Finance Ministry by prescribed dates before year-end
Savings and unutilized provisions must be surrendered immediately, not held for future excesses
Rush of expenditure in closing months is a breach of financial propriety and must be avoided
Financial Advisers must ensure adherence to Quarterly Expenditure Plan and related guidelines

00bff883147e2eca26893c0317f640319d5ad43c.rule_statement: No expenditure on New Service not in Annual Budget without supplementary grant or Contingency Fund advance
Subordinate authority must not exceed allotment; obtain additional allotment if excess is anticipated
Disbursing Officer cannot authorize excess payments without administrative authority's orders
00bff883147e2eca26893c0317f640319d5ad43c.ministry_must_show: Administrative authority's approval for excess claims

00bff883147e2eca26893c0317f640319d5ad43c.rule_statement: Re-appropriation of funds within a grant may be sanctioned by competent authority before financial year ends
Re-appropriation only if full utilization of original appropriation is not expected

8b3c1aa8ae8b03833320a7a6335a9562bbe21b86.rule_statement: Funds cannot be re-appropriated from a unit to restore diverted funds later in the year
Re-appropriation applications must include Form GFR 1 and state reasons for savings and excess over Rs. 1 lakh
8b3c1aa8ae8b03833320a7a6335a9562bbe21b86.ministry_must_show: Complete application with required forms and reasons

8b3c1aa8ae8b03833320a7a6335a9562bbe21b86.rule_statement: Indisputably payable government money should not be left unpaid
Provisions for anticipated liabilities must be included in Demands for Grants presented to Parliament
The Chief Accounting Authority is responsible for financial management of their Ministry/Department

94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.rule_statement: Ministry or Department must manage finances responsibly and accountably.
Ensure public funds are used for intended purposes.
94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.ministry_must_show: Evidence of fund allocation and usage

94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.rule_statement: Use resources effectively, efficiently, and transparently to meet project objectives.
94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.ministry_must_show: Reports on resource utilization and performance

94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.rule_statement: Appear before Public Accounts and other Parliamentary Committees for examination.
Regularly review and monitor program performance to achieve objectives.
Prepare expenditure statements as per regulations and directives.
Maintain proper records of financial transactions and internal controls.
Follow government procurement procedures for works and services transparently.
Ensure collection of all dues and avoid unauthorized or wasteful expenditure.
94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.ministry_must_show: Reports on dues collected and expenditure audits

94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.rule_statement: Prepare annual accounts showing receipts, disbursements, and liabilities.
94f4244ec6a1a193d7e915f5c093c0b083e2f419.ministry_must_show: Submit accounts to President within six months

cdaa99c44f36833d179da18bddede3f8f810b544.rule_statement: Union Government accounts must follow the form prescribed by the President on CAG's advice
Government accounts must adhere to principles in Government Accounting Rules, 1990 and related manuals
cdaa99c44f36833d179da18bddede3f8f810b544.ministry_must_show: Adherence to accounting rules and manuals

cdaa99c44f36833d179da18bddede3f8f810b544.rule_statement: Government accounts must be prepared on a cash basis, with limited exceptions
Annual accounts must record transactions from 1st April to 31st March
Government accounts must be maintained in Indian Rupees; foreign transactions converted

ff22109f2f354e0514895d2e1d3200a0e80abd21.rule_statement: Government accounts divided into Consolidated Fund, Contingency Fund, and Public Account with specific structures.
ff22109f2f354e0514895d2e1d3200a0e80abd21.ministry_must_show: Adherence to account classification and structure

ff22109f2f354e0514895d2e1d3200a0e80abd21.rule_statement: Transactions classified by functions and activities, not by department; structured into Major, Minor, Sub, and Detailed Heads.

5a8d02c15b287d129d1e7c371ef8d5e21085c907.rule_statement: New Heads of Account can be opened by DoE on CAG's advice under Article 150
5a8d02c15b287d129d1e7c371ef8d5e21085c907.ministry_must_show: Consultation with CAG and adherence to guidelines

5a8d02c15b287d129d1e7c371ef8d5e21085c907.rule_statement: Budget Heads must align with prescribed classification rules in estimates and orders
5a8d02c15b287d129d1e7c371ef8d5e21085c907.ministry_must_show: Compliance with classification rules in budget documents

5a8d02c15b287d129d1e7c371ef8d5e21085c907.rule_statement: Officers must maintain accurate accounts of receipts and expenditures as prescribed
5a8d02c15b287d129d1e7c371ef8d5e21085c907.ministry_must_show: Timely submission of accounts and returns

0113004ea6e4750a0b2446ebef32dc9b7527c5c3.rule_statement: Drawing Officers must record classification on all bills and challans; refer doubts to Principal Accounts Officer
0113004ea6e4750a0b2446ebef32dc9b7527c5c3.ministry_must_show: Proper classification on all financial documents

0113004ea6e4750a0b2446ebef32dc9b7527c5c3.rule_statement: Charged expenditure is not subject to legislative vote; Voted expenditure must be separately shown in accounts and budgets
Expenditure for acquiring permanent tangible assets classified as Capital Expenditure
RBI acts as banker to Government, maintaining cash balance and providing banking facilities; payments through identified branches
0113004ea6e4750a0b2446ebef32dc9b7527c5c3.ministry_must_show: Payments processed through Accredited Bank branches

e7762538d5ca9bd152bfdf1fe1c4f0c9e64743dd.rule_statement: Follow RBI's Memoranda for remittance of government receipts and reimbursement payments
Use PFMS for sanction preparation, bill processing, and financial reporting
e7762538d5ca9bd152bfdf1fe1c4f0c9e64743dd.ministry_must_show: Evidence of PFMS utilization in financial processes

e7762538d5ca9bd152bfdf1fe1c4f0c9e64743dd.rule_statement: All ministries must register implementing agencies for grant-in-aid
Direct benefits must be transferred to beneficiaries using ICT, minimizing intermediaries
DBT includes both cash and in-kind transfers to beneficiaries and enablers
Cash benefits must be transferred directly or through designated accounts/agencies
In-kind transfers must be provided to individuals or households as per scheme guidelines

d274e28202b33253e07d6beaf5eef916e7233b09.rule_statement: Appropriation Accounts must be prepared by Principal Accounts Officers and signed by Chief Accounting Authorities
d274e28202b33253e07d6beaf5eef916e7233b09.ministry_must_show: Appropriation Accounts prepared under guidance of Controller General of Accounts

d274e28202b33253e07d6beaf5eef916e7233b09.rule_statement: Annual Finance Accounts showing receipts and disbursements must be prepared and signed by the Controller
Annual accounts must be prepared on agreed dates and in prescribed forms, submitted for certification
d274e28202b33253e07d6beaf5eef916e7233b09.ministry_must_show: Certified Annual Accounts submitted to the President

63ad8d85288b65bf69873259e839612f5cff48a1.rule_statement: Administrative Ministries must disclose financial stakes in PPPs, PSCs, JVs in their Annual Report
63ad8d85288b65bf69873259e839612f5cff48a1.ministry_must_show: Details of financial stakes in Annual Report

63ad8d85288b65bf69873259e839612f5cff48a1.rule_statement: Departments with commercial activities must maintain subsidiary proforma accounts as agreed with CAG
Subsidiary accounts must follow methods and principles set by Government orders
63ad8d85288b65bf69873259e839612f5cff48a1.ministry_must_show: Compliance with Government orders on accounting

63ad8d85288b65bf69873259e839612f5cff48a1.rule_statement: Personal Deposit Accounts require special order from Ministry after ensuring proper maintenance and audit
63ad8d85288b65bf69873259e839612f5cff48a1.ministry_must_show: Satisfactory arrangement for account maintenance and audit

392c3f058d9c4959731623f34c2916d004e0424b.rule_statement: Regulations must ensure accurate cost deductions from commercial accounts maintained for assessment.
Heads of units must obtain Government orders for subsidiary accounts and submit them to the Accounts Officer.
392c3f058d9c4959731623f34c2916d004e0424b.ministry_must_show: Orders from Government on account nature and form

392c3f058d9c4959731623f34c2916d004e0424b.rule_statement: Personal Deposit Accounts allow designated officers to manage receipts and withdrawals under bank oversight.
392c3f058d9c4959731623f34c2916d004e0424b.ministry_must_show: Maintain personal ledger to prevent negative balance

8f7e4c94311fe4735edb0bd7b457803ed9143b51.rule_statement: Personal Deposit accounts can be opened for Designated Officers managing wards and estates, with audit arrangements required.
Personal Deposit accounts for Civil and Criminal Courts’ deposits must be in favor of the Chief Judicial Authority.
Accounts can be opened for regulatory receipts credited to a Fund or Account without involving the Consolidated Fund.
8f7e4c94311fe4735edb0bd7b457803ed9143b51.ministry_must_show: Evidence of regulatory activity and fund usage

8f7e4c94311fe4735edb0bd7b457803ed9143b51.rule_statement: Accounts required by law or rules with liabilities on the Government can be opened.
Defence officers can open Personal Deposit accounts for managing public funds.
Revenue must cover maintenance and working expenses for capital expenditure projects.
8f7e4c94311fe4735edb0bd7b457803ed9143b51.ministry_must_show: Budget for maintenance and working expenses

572c0ef73a9c97b487ba54fc7a7040c87d10372c.rule_statement: Revenue must cover depreciation of capital assets; only genuine improvements can be charged to Capital.
572c0ef73a9c97b487ba54fc7a7040c87d10372c.ministry_must_show: Justification for capital charges on improvements

572c0ef73a9c97b487ba54fc7a7040c87d10372c.rule_statement: Expenditure for damage repair from calamities charged based on asset creation or restoration as determined by Government.
Temporary asset expenditure not capital unless authorized by the President on CAG's advice.
Capital covers initial construction, equipment, and maintenance charges until service opening; also genuine improvements.
572c0ef73a9c97b487ba54fc7a7040c87d10372c.ministry_must_show: Compliance with rules for capital charges

530fc3c5078efbd18dea8b078d4bc418a71f1254.rule_statement: Loan conversions may be authorized by the President on CAG's advice and debited to a Capital Head
530fc3c5078efbd18dea8b078d4bc418a71f1254.ministry_must_show: Approval from President and CAG's advice

530fc3c5078efbd18dea8b078d4bc418a71f1254.rule_statement: Allocation between capital and revenue expenditure must follow government orders after consulting CAG
Interest rates for capital accounts must be specified by the government, except in special cases
Interest on capital outlay from specific loans charged at prescribed rates based on actual loan rates
530fc3c5078efbd18dea8b078d4bc418a71f1254.ministry_must_show: Details of loan rates and charges

530fc3c5078efbd18dea8b078d4bc418a71f1254.rule_statement: Interest calculated on previous year's capital outlay plus half of current year's outlay
Capitalized interest during construction must be written back as first charge on capital receipts
530fc3c5078efbd18dea8b078d4bc418a71f1254.ministry_must_show: Records of capitalized interest and receipts

f3dd48612f5d1d53cf6f1eeed9a995bbf5b8f72e.rule_statement: Union Territories and States won't make petty claims under ₹10,000 against each other
Financial adjustments with States must follow mutual agreements and specific rules in Appendix-5
Re-audit of past transactions is accepted every three years for classification errors
Adjustments must be made unless otherwise agreed upon

ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.rule_statement: Adjustments must be made unless agreed otherwise, especially for commercial departments or similar transactions.
ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.ministry_must_show: Agreement documentation if adjustments not made

ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.rule_statement: Central Government shall not prefer petty and isolated claims for services rendered.
ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.ministry_must_show: Evidence of claim nature and justification

ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.rule_statement: Claims must be petty, occasional, and for services rendered, not supplies, to be covered by reciprocal arrangements.
ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.ministry_must_show: Classification of claims as petty and occasional

ab531109ec25fddbbefa709306db4f80a3698f47.rule_statement: For joint projects, recoveries from other Governments must be shown as abatement of charges in accounts.
Claims by State Governments for extra agency costs under Article 258 will follow President's directions.
General principles must be observed when dealing with claims from State Governments under Article 258.

a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.rule_statement: State Commercial Departments can charge normal commercial costs for agency work
PWD agency costs represented by agreed percentage charges on Central Works costs
a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.ministry_must_show: Agreement on percentage charges with State Government

a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.rule_statement: Cost of joint establishment shared based on fixed annual sums agreed with State Government
Claims details from State Governments must be ascertained and compared with past charges
a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.ministry_must_show: Comparison of current and past charges

a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.rule_statement: If charges ≤ ₹50,000, offer a five-year contract with fixed annual payment
If charges > ₹50,000, annual statement of proposed charges required during Budget prep
Ministry of Finance will arrange arbitration in exceptional cases
Consult Ministry of Finance on all matters under Article 258(3)
a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.ministry_must_show: Consultation records with Ministry of Finance

a8ec619743eaf352deb591560142f385dd844b43.rule_statement: Follow principles for transactions related to agency functions under Article 258

97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.rule_statement: Expenditure on extra staff or contingencies is reimbursable under Article 258(3) of the Constitution
97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.ministry_must_show: State Budget provision and adjustment under normal Heads of Accounts

97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.rule_statement: Leave and pensionary charges can be included in the extra cost if state rules allow
97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.ministry_must_show: Compliance with state service and financial rules

97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.rule_statement: Expenditure on work like National Highways is adjusted directly in Central Government accounts
97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.ministry_must_show: No inclusion in State Budget or vote required

97545b9755d31bd8cd7a483876b3888e8334016d.rule_statement: Expenditure will be adjusted under '8658 – Suspense Accounts – PAO Suspense' pending clearance

8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.rule_statement: Follow Rule 116 for entrusting State functions to Central Government; budget for extra costs must be provided
8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.ministry_must_show: Budget inclusion for extra costs and recovery plan

8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.rule_statement: Inter-Governmental adjustments allowed until April 10 or specified date by Controller General of Accounts
Services to foreign governments or bodies require payment unless exempted by Government orders
8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.ministry_must_show: Proof of payment or exemption order

8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.rule_statement: Recoveries for services to non-Government parties classified as Government receipts
Full cost of services as agent for private bodies must be recovered to ensure no net cost to Government
8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.ministry_must_show: Evidence of cost recovery from private body

8c766c328ce24632e1523453e6824781ed62505b.rule_statement: Payments for services to outside bodies should be made through grant-in-aid, not by remission
Charges for maintenance and boundary disputes must be clearly defined and managed

7462efc66fedae26f7eb35fe55c6b71a81cd8515.rule_statement: Half of the maintenance charges for boundary disputes are borne by the Central Government, with potential recovery from the foreign country.
7462efc66fedae26f7eb35fe55c6b71a81cd8515.ministry_must_show: Documentation of charges and recovery efforts

7462efc66fedae26f7eb35fe55c6b71a81cd8515.rule_statement: Each government bears maintenance costs of boundary lines on its side; costs for survey marks are similarly borne by the respective government.
7462efc66fedae26f7eb35fe55c6b71a81cd8515.ministry_must_show: Records of maintenance costs and agreements

7462efc66fedae26f7eb35fe55c6b71a81cd8515.rule_statement: Special arrangements for boundary maintenance with Nepal will be established through consultation.
Bhutan's share of boundary costs will currently be covered by the Central Government.
Adjustments for past year accounts not allowed unless reasonably anticipated; the onus is on the Controlling Officer.

5fd3e1cf4550941616c36335cfdbe3a777615e61.rule_statement: Recoveries for services between Departments are deductions from gross expenditure, except for Commercial Departments
Recoveries by Commercial Departments are treated as receipts unless acting as agents for non-core functions
5fd3e1cf4550941616c36335cfdbe3a777615e61.ministry_must_show: Clarification on function type for recoveries

5fd3e1cf4550941616c36335cfdbe3a777615e61.rule_statement: Recoveries of fees by Central Purchase Organizations are treated as receipts of the concerned Department
Recovery denotes repayment by one Department for charges incurred by another, classified as final expenditure
Recoveries classified as deductions must be shown as 'below the line' in Demand for Grant
5fd3e1cf4550941616c36335cfdbe3a777615e61.ministry_must_show: Inclusion in Demand for Grant documentation

5fd3e1cf4550941616c36335cfdbe3a777615e61.rule_statement: Pensionary liability for commercial departments assessed on a contribution basis at rates fixed by Government
Departments without regular accounts must include pension liabilities in overhead and manufacturing cost estimates

e28dc8c5365d5b4ffe66fcb96f9038d7304d4247.rule_statement: Railways, Posts, and Defence are separate for pensionary charge adjustments.
Pension liability for commercial departments charged based on 15th-year service percentage.
Original works include new constructions and alterations; minor works add value without creating new assets.
Administrative control includes responsibility for construction, maintenance, and fund provision.
e28dc8c5365d5b4ffe66fcb96f9038d7304d4247.ministry_must_show: Evidence of responsibility and fund allocation

e28dc8c5365d5b4ffe66fcb96f9038d7304d4247.rule_statement: Sanction powers for works regulated by Delegation of Financial Powers Rules.
Ministries may execute repair works up to 60 Lakhs after due procedure.

dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.rule_statement: Ministries can execute repair works up to Rs 60 Lakhs after following due procedures in specified rules
Ministries may assign repair works above Rs 60 Lakhs to authorized Public Works Organisations
dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.ministry_must_show: List of assigned PWOs and justification

dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.rule_statement: Ministries can award works above Rs 60 Lakhs to PSUs or notified organizations after ensuring competition
dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.ministry_must_show: Evidence of competition and evaluation criteria

dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.rule_statement: In exceptional cases, works can be awarded on a nomination basis under specific conditions
dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.ministry_must_show: Justification for nomination and compliance with Rule 194

dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.rule_statement: Scientific Ministries can assign repair works up to Rs 5 crore on nomination basis to specified organizations
dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.ministry_must_show: List of organizations and compliance with GFRs

dccbd7240b8bfd48628cf93ca26ad9761d1192ef.rule_statement: Special provision for scientific ministries valid until 31.03.2025, subject to review by DoE

fb9c341fe2b9d75c3099b90ff22dd9a66365dc34.rule_statement: Works under Public Works Departments must be funded solely from Civil Works budget, not mixed with departmental funds.
fb9c341fe2b9d75c3099b90ff22dd9a66365dc34.ministry_must_show: Clear funding allocation for Civil Works

fb9c341fe2b9d75c3099b90ff22dd9a66365dc34.rule_statement: Initiation and execution of works must follow departmental regulations and special orders.
Empowered project teams should be established for large projects, focused solely on execution.
No work may start until administrative approval, expenditure sanction, detailed design, estimates, funds, tenders, and work order are secured.

28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.rule_statement: In urgent cases, an executive officer can incur liabilities without prior compliance but must seek approval afterward
28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.ministry_must_show: Approval from competent authority and notify Accounts Officer

28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.rule_statement: Any necessary project development during ongoing work requires a supplementary estimate
A group of works is treated as one project for approvals, regardless of individual costs
28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.ministry_must_show: Approval for the entire project group, not just individual works

28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.rule_statement: Anticipated or actual savings from a project cannot be used for additional work without special authority
28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.ministry_must_show: Special authority for any additional work

28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.rule_statement: Ministries must follow prescribed procedures for executing works, based on departmental regulations
28d546406010a62dd5700ff96c82a3659cf93456.ministry_must_show: Adhere to departmental regulations and consult Accounts Officer

18bcbd90874011f8d66c16bf2fb50630501c70aa.rule_statement: For projects costing Rs. 100 crore or more, a Review Committee must be established for progress review
Review Committee can accept variations within 10% of approved estimates for projects over Rs. 100 crore
For projects under Rs. 100 crore, Administrative Ministry can decide on review mechanisms and variations
Detailed design and estimates must be prepared before any work sanction
No work can start without Administrative Approval and Expenditure Sanction based on estimates
18bcbd90874011f8d66c16bf2fb50630501c70aa.ministry_must_show: Administrative Approval and Expenditure Sanction documents

18bcbd90874011f8d66c16bf2fb50630501c70aa.rule_statement: Open tenders must be called for works costing between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 60 lakh
Limited tenders are required for works costing less than Rs. 10 lakh
Contract Agreement must be executed before starting any work
Final payment requires a Personal Certificate from the Officer-in-charge confirming work execution
Administrative Approval and Expenditure Sanction required for original/minor works as per financial delegation rules
Review of projects must be conducted after project completion

a4e74fa714aa7fb03096685495c45352d5765bb6.rule_statement: Projects must be reviewed after completion as per DoE guidelines
General rules for procurement of goods apply to all Ministries/Departments
Goods include various items and services related to procurement, excluding library materials
Authorities must ensure efficiency, economy, and transparency in public procurement
a4e74fa714aa7fb03096685495c45352d5765bb6.ministry_must_show: Demonstrate accountability and fair treatment in procurement

41d7ba99ad2149fffbeaafdb9133f2deabd9d5c3.rule_statement: Procurement descriptions must be objective, functional, and measurable, specifying characteristics.
Specifications must not require specific trademarks or brands.
Specifications must clearly define quality, type, and quantity based on organizational needs.
Technical specifications should follow national or international standards where applicable.
Avoid purchasing excess quantities to minimize inventory costs.
Offers must be invited through a fair, transparent, and reasonable process.
Procuring authority must ensure selected offers meet requirements and price is reasonable.
Publish complete procurement cycle schedule from tender issue to contract date.
Annual Procurement Plan must be prepared and published on the website before the year starts.

1d084b8065dea5436de7e5aea2a8c6bda3bdb862.rule_statement: All Ministries/Departments must prepare and publish an Annual Procurement Plan before the year starts
DoE can impose procurement restrictions based on national security concerns
Authorities can sanction purchases for public service as per Delegation of Financial Powers Rules
Procurement during military operations regulated by special rules issued by the Government
1d084b8065dea5436de7e5aea2a8c6bda3bdb862.ministry_must_show: Compliance with special orders for military procurement

1d084b8065dea5436de7e5aea2a8c6bda3bdb862.rule_statement: Ministries can procure goods/services not available on GeM; mandatory for common use items
Mandatory procurement of common use goods/services through GeM; suppliers' credentials must be certified

d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.rule_statement: Procure up to Rs.50,000 through GeM suppliers meeting quality and delivery standards; no limit for automobiles.
d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.ministry_must_show: Compliance with quality and delivery specifications

d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.rule_statement: For Rs.50,000 to Rs.10,00,000, procure from lowest-priced GeM seller among three manufacturers, using online bidding if needed.
d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.ministry_must_show: Evidence of lowest price and manufacturer diversity

d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.rule_statement: Above Rs.10,00,000, procure from lowest-priced supplier after mandatory bids using GeM tools.
Monetary ceiling applies only to GeM purchases; GFR rules apply for outside GeM purchases.
d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.ministry_must_show: Compliance with GFR for non-GeM purchases

d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.rule_statement: Ministries must prepare procurement plans on OPEX or CAPEX model and project on GeM within 30 days of Budget approval.
d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.ministry_must_show: Annual Procurement Plan submitted on GeM

d5e03b4a3f1cf4f24e5cc0bae45ac2a62f597f5e.rule_statement: Government Buyers must check price reasonableness using GeM's Business Analytics tools before ordering.

31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.rule_statement: Do not divide demand for goods into smaller quantities to avoid L-1 procurement rules
31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.ministry_must_show: Justification for demand quantity and procurement method

31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.rule_statement: Suppliers for goods not on GeM must be registered following a fair and transparent process
31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.ministry_must_show: Documentation of registration process and publicity

31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.rule_statement: Verify supplier credentials, capability, and past performance before registration
Registered suppliers must renew registration every 1 to 3 years; new suppliers can apply anytime
31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.ministry_must_show: Renewal applications and new supplier evaluations

31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.rule_statement: Monitor performance of registered suppliers; they can be removed for non-compliance
31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.ministry_must_show: Records of supplier performance and compliance checks

31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.rule_statement: Exhibit the list of registered suppliers on procurement websites or e-Procurement portals
31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.ministry_must_show: Updated supplier list on relevant platforms

31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.rule_statement: Debar bidders convicted of corruption or causing public harm during procurement execution
31da2f4b784c0259cde0247faaa9837e5f1338f0.ministry_must_show: Records of convictions and debarment decisions

de98811598bc877ee8169bf1869e5d7b417f7e60.rule_statement: Debarred bidders cannot participate in procurement for up to three years from debarment date
de98811598bc877ee8169bf1869e5d7b417f7e60.ministry_must_show: List of debarred bidders on procurement portal

de98811598bc877ee8169bf1869e5d7b417f7e60.rule_statement: Procuring entity may debar a bidder for up to two years for breaching the code of integrity
de98811598bc877ee8169bf1869e5d7b417f7e60.ministry_must_show: List of debarred bidders on department website

de98811598bc877ee8169bf1869e5d7b417f7e60.rule_statement: Bidders must be given a chance to represent against debarment before action is taken
Ministries may enlist Indian agents to quote on behalf of foreign principals
Mandatory procurement of 20% handloom items from KVIC and related entities for textiles
MSME procurement policy established under MSME Development Act, 2006
Central Government may mandate procurement from specific bidder categories to promote local goods/services
Goods purchases up to Rs. 50,000 can be made without quotations, with a competent authority's certificate

e186b4eda38a2b24744930a8844ca5fd77f73b16.rule_statement: Goods above Rs.50,000 and up to Rs.5,00,000 may be purchased on Local Purchase Committee's recommendation
e186b4eda38a2b24744930a8844ca5fd77f73b16.ministry_must_show: Recommendations from the Local Purchase Committee

e186b4eda38a2b24744930a8844ca5fd77f73b16.rule_statement: Scientific Ministries can purchase goods up to Rs.1,00,000 without GeM availability
Procurement powers apply only if goods are unavailable on GeM

13f4ce30e9d93239d39e8dae6ca56853c97ec6d1.rule_statement: Goods costing Rs. 1,00,000 to Rs. 10,00,000 can be purchased by specified Scientific Ministries if not available on GeM
Do not divide demand into smaller quantities to avoid higher authority sanction
Goods must be procured via bids unless specified otherwise in Rules 154 and 155
13f4ce30e9d93239d39e8dae6ca56853c97ec6d1.ministry_must_show: Bidding documentation as per standard methods

13f4ce30e9d93239d39e8dae6ca56853c97ec6d1.rule_statement: E-Publishing requirements must be adhered to for procurement processes

b1b80091b869caac4f58b0a7033b9df82d659c7f.rule_statement: All tender enquiries and bid awards must be published on the CPPP by all Central Government entities
Confidential cases exempt from e-publishing require Secretary's approval and quarterly reporting to DoE
E-publishing instructions apply to all forms of tender enquiries and proposals
b1b80091b869caac4f58b0a7033b9df82d659c7f.ministry_must_show: Compliance with e-publishing for all notices

b1b80091b869caac4f58b0a7033b9df82d659c7f.rule_statement: E-publishing rules do not apply to procurements under Rules 154 and 155 of GFR
All bids must be received through e-procurement portals for all procurements
Ministries with low procurement volume may not need to use e-procurement

d313ebf465242762e964f666b53ee4563789811e.rule_statement: Tenders for goods valued at Rs. 50 lakhs and above must be advertised on GeM and CPPP
d313ebf465242762e964f666b53ee4563789811e.ministry_must_show: Proof of advertisement on GeM and CPPP

d313ebf465242762e964f666b53ee4563789811e.rule_statement: Complete bidding documents must be posted on the organization's website and CPPP
Advertisements must include the web address for downloading bidding documents
For GTE, tender notices may be sent to Indian and Foreign Embassies if local goods are unavailable
GTE not allowed for tenders up to Rs 200 crore unless justified and approved
d313ebf465242762e964f666b53ee4563789811e.ministry_must_show: Detailed justification and approval from Competent Authority

d313ebf465242762e964f666b53ee4563789811e.rule_statement: No cost for tender documents downloaded by bidders to promote participation
Minimum bid submission time is three weeks from tender notice publication or document availability
d313ebf465242762e964f666b53ee4563789811e.ministry_must_show: Evidence of compliance with submission time

6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.rule_statement: Limited Tender Enquiry allowed for goods up to ₹50 Lakhs; must send documents to registered suppliers
6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.ministry_must_show: List of more than three registered suppliers

6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.rule_statement: Unsolicited bids not accepted; firms must register for future tenders
6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.ministry_must_show: System for firm registration for future tenders

6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.rule_statement: Limited Tender Enquiry can be used for urgent needs over ₹50 Lakhs with competent authority's certification
6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.ministry_must_show: Certification of urgency and justification for non-advertised tender

6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.rule_statement: Limited Tender Enquiry can be used if public interest justifies non-advertised tender, with written reasons
6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.ministry_must_show: Written reasons for public interest decision

6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.rule_statement: Limited Tender Enquiry can be used if supply sources are known and no new sources likely
6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.ministry_must_show: Written record of known supply sources

6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.rule_statement: Sufficient time must be allowed for bid submissions in Limited Tender Enquiry
Two bid system required for high value, complex machinery purchases
6f07b77a88cf9a440a219faaf2eb01a368a6c449.ministry_must_show: Separate technical and financial bid submissions

d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.rule_statement: Obtain bids in two stages: technical bids first, then financial bids for acceptable offers
d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.ministry_must_show: Sealed technical and financial bids in separate covers

d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.rule_statement: Use two-stage bidding if detailed specifications can't be formulated without bidder input
Use two-stage bidding if procurement subject to rapid technological changes or market fluctuations
d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.ministry_must_show: Analysis of technological and market factors

d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.rule_statement: Use two-stage bidding for contracts related to research, study, or development without commercial production
d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.ministry_must_show: Details of research or development purpose

d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.rule_statement: Use two-stage bidding if bidders must conduct surveys or risk assessments for procurement
d0e46460249809d32018574f8753b4ea6da05d4d.ministry_must_show: Survey and assessment plan from bidders

5d174a62fa00b7f3479ebc99a82aa9710338d26f.rule_statement: First stage bids must be invited without bid prices, focusing on technical aspects and terms
Eligible first stage bids evaluated by a committee constituted by the Ministry/Department
Committee may hold discussions with bidders, ensuring equal opportunity for all
Terms and conditions can be revised without altering the fundamental nature of procurement
Second stage invites bids from non-rejected first stage bidders with revised terms
5d174a62fa00b7f3479ebc99a82aa9710338d26f.ministry_must_show: Invitation for final bids with updated terms

5d174a62fa00b7f3479ebc99a82aa9710338d26f.rule_statement: Bidders can withdraw without penalty if unable to meet revised specifications
Late bids received after the deadline will not be considered
Single source procurement allowed under specific circumstances with proper documentation
Emergency purchases must be sourced from a specific supplier with recorded reasons
Standardization requires procurement from a selected firm based on expert advice

537e2039be8b890179fe7adf25a97b55623f20af.rule_statement: Proprietary Article Certificate required before single-source procurement under sub-Rule 166
Electronic Reverse Auction is an online method for selecting bids with real-time evaluation
Criteria for successful bid must be quantifiable and monetary
Procedure for electronic reverse auction must include solicitation and auction details
537e2039be8b890179fe7adf25a97b55623f20af.ministry_must_show: Auction invitation with all required details

537e2039be8b890179fe7adf25a97b55623f20af.rule_statement: Bidding document must include all terms, conditions, and stipulations in specified chapters
537e2039be8b890179fe7adf25a97b55623f20af.ministry_must_show: Bidding document with all necessary chapters

17646d8bae5df9587756271e09b233c045ba11a6.rule_statement: Bidding documents must include specified chapters detailing instructions, conditions, requirements, specifications, price schedule, and forms.
17646d8bae5df9587756271e09b233c045ba11a6.ministry_must_show: Complete bidding document with all required chapters

17646d8bae5df9587756271e09b233c045ba11a6.rule_statement: Maintenance contracts may be needed based on cost and nature of goods, especially for costly equipment.

50fa43b1868c4422a5ab911382b8d12e7f2877b9.rule_statement: Bid Security required from bidders, except MSEs and registered Startups, to prevent bid withdrawal or alteration
50fa43b1868c4422a5ab911382b8d12e7f2877b9.ministry_must_show: Specify bid security amount in bidding documents

50fa43b1868c4422a5ab911382b8d12e7f2877b9.rule_statement: Return bid securities of unsuccessful bidders within 30 days after contract award or technical evaluation results
50fa43b1868c4422a5ab911382b8d12e7f2877b9.ministry_must_show: Documented return process for bid securities

50fa43b1868c4422a5ab911382b8d12e7f2877b9.rule_statement: Bidders may sign a declaration instead of providing Bid Security, agreeing to penalties for withdrawal or failure to contract
50fa43b1868c4422a5ab911382b8d12e7f2877b9.ministry_must_show: Include declaration clause in bid documents

a85511dd964ec206146698531d0ceddde845e70f.rule_statement: Performance Security required from successful bidders, varies by market conditions for goods
Performance Security amount should be 3-5% for non-consultancy services
a85511dd964ec206146698531d0ceddde845e70f.ministry_must_show: Documented calculation of Performance Security percentage

a85511dd964ec206146698531d0ceddde845e70f.rule_statement: Performance Security must be valid for 60 days beyond contract completion and warranty obligations
Bid security refunded upon receipt of Performance Security from successful bidder
Ordinarily, payments should be post-service, but advance payments allowed under specific conditions
Advance payments capped at 30% for private firms, 40% for government agencies
a85511dd964ec206146698531d0ceddde845e70f.ministry_must_show: Contract value documentation for advance payment calculation

a85511dd964ec206146698531d0ceddde845e70f.rule_statement: Ensure transparency, competition, and fairness in procurement to eliminate arbitrariness

fd159fbd3facea3302d23d00123d13f53de1c4c2.rule_statement: All government purchases must be transparent, competitive, and fair to ensure best value for money.
Bidding documents must be clear, comprehensive, and free of ambiguities.
